_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average index of global commodity prices for coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice benchmarked to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 141. This reflects a roughly 41% increase since 2015, highlighting a notable but uneven rise. The climb in prices has been uneven across the components, with some commodities contributing more significantly to the overall increase. This basket is a summary measure, not a uniform increase across all items.
notes.movers: Coffee has nearly doubled in price since 2015, up 99%, making it the largest contributor to the rise in the basket. Orange juice prices have increased by 34%, wheat by 19%, and sugar by 13%. These changes are driven more by localized weather events and crop diseases, such as frost and drought affecting arabica coffee and citrus greening impacting orange juice, rather than broad-based inflation. The markets for these soft commodities are relatively thin, meaning that supply disruptions can cause sharp price fluctuations.
notes.why: The headline increase in the breakfast basket is largely attributable to a few weather-sensitive crops vulnerable to disease and adverse climate conditions. Given the nature of these soft commodity markets, price levels can remain elevated for extended periods if adverse conditions persist. This underlines the importance of considering environmental factors when analyzing fluctuations in such commodity prices.
